title: Accessible Names, Images, Language, Headings, Landmarks, and Human Review · Transfer Build
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers accessible names, images, language, headings, landmarks, and human review into a changed accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff with automated and judgment evidence. Lea…
objectives: Check declared page and part language, document title presence and purpose, and conflicts with observed content while retaining human-review limits.
Extract headings, labels, sections, and landmarks in document context and flag missing or misleading structure without enforcing a simplistic numeric ladder.
Classify informative, decorative, functional, complex, grouped, linked, and text images and assess alternative evidence in context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Page Language Title
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 6412 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to audit language, title, structure, images, names, and relationships with evidence and mandatory human review; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Automated Accessibility Limit
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit-xdk55z
# Competency: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit.
# Case 3002: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_automated_accessibility_limit_xdk55z(pages: int = 24, automated_findings: int = 38, manual_checks: int = 42, conformance_claims: int = 0, findings_with_evidence: int = 38) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xdk55z = "xdk55z-z55kdx-3002"
    assert evidence_variant_xdk55z
    evidence_axes_xdk55z = (
        "authorizationxdk55z",
        "url3002",
        "responsez55kdx",
        "treexdk55z3002",
        "frontier3002xdk55z",
        "reportz55kdx3002",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xdk55z)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or automated_findings < 0:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-count"
    if findings_with_evidence != automated_findings:
        return False, findings_with_evidence, "finding-evidence"
    if manual_checks < pages:
        return False, manual_checks, "manual-review-gap"
    if conformance_claims > 0:
        return False, conformance_claims, "conformance-overclaim"
    assert findings_with_evidence == automated_findings and conformance_claims == 0
    return True, automated_findings, "bounded-accessibility-audit"
